ops.title: For protocol/format integrations in cc-notes (and Go tools generally), implement natively in-process — no synthetic commits, no shelling to helper binaries; exec only for credentialed transport
ops.body:
While planning git-lfs-backed attachments for cc-notes, the user rejected a design that committed pointer files into entity trees (for `git lfs` ref-scan discovery) and shelled out to the `git-lfs` binary: *"wherever possible do the 'native' thing for git-lfs rather than just make fake commits or shell out or whatever, just like we do the 'native' thing with other git objects/refs."*

**Why:** cc-notes' architecture is the precedent — `internal/gitobj` manipulates objects/refs in-process via go-git; `internal/gitcmd` execs `git` *only* where user credentials/transport demand it. Fabricated artifacts (fake commits, helper-binary invocations) exist to satisfy someone else's discovery mechanism; when we own both ends of a flow, speak the protocol directly.

**How to apply:** When integrating a format or protocol (LFS pointer files, batch API, object stores), implement the codec/store/client in Go in-process; keep on-disk layouts standard for interop, but never require the third-party binary at runtime. Exec concessions are allowed only for credential/transport plumbing (`git credential fill`, `ssh <host> git-lfs-authenticate`), mirroring [[prefers-go-release-binaries-for-agent-tools]]'s self-contained-binary ethos.
